items.headline: Malaysia Picks a Former Judge to Lead Its Anti-Graft Agency
items.summary: Malaysia named Abdul Halim Bin Aman as the next anti-graft chief, taking over from embattled head Azam Baki when his term ends next month.

items.headline: China Hit Brakes on Fiscal Stimulus as Economy Holds Up Amid War
items.summary: China pulled back on fiscal spending in March as the economy rebounded at the start of the year despite disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

items.headline: US Sanctions Giant Chinese Refiner Hengli, Ships for Iran Links
items.summary: The US sanctioned a large Chinese private oil refiner, Hengli Petrochemical (Dalian) Refinery Co., for its Iranian links, ramping up economic pressure on Tehran ahead of potential diplomatic talks in the coming days.

items.headline: AI Chip Surge Elevates Taiwan, Korea in Global Equity Rankings
items.summary: The artificial intelligence boom has triggered a seismic reshuffling of global equity markets, with Taiwan and South Korea muscling past European nations one by one.

items.headline: Kentucky's Thomas Massie Stakes His 'MAGA' Claim
items.summary: Kentucky Representative Thomas Massie, a committed libertarian, is the rare Republican willing to publicly criticize Donald Trump—over domestic spending, the war in Iran, the president’s efforts to bury the Jeffrey Epstein files. And he’s done this while running for an eighth term in Kentucky’s deep-red 4th Congressional District, which gave Trump two-thirds of the vote in 2024. Bloomberg Businessweek National Correspondent Josh Green joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Grindr CEO on Inaugural WHCD Party, Political Initiatives
items.summary: Grindr CEO George Arison joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the dating app's inaugural White House Correspondents' Dinner party, plus the company's presence in Washington, DC focused on HIV treatment and other health initiatives. Arison also discusses the company's newest tiers, including AI features. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Mid-Market Investment Bank Lincoln International Files for IPO
items.summary: Lincoln International Inc., a mid-market investment bank that advises business owners and private equity funds, filed for a US initial public offering, disclosing growing net income.

items.headline: Intel Plans Multi-Billion Dollar Bond Sale Tied to Apollo Deal
items.summary: Intel Corp. is readying a multi-billion dollar bond sale to finance the repurchase of half of a plant in Ireland that it had previously sold to Apollo Global Management Inc.

items.headline: Ricky Sandler Shuttering Eminence Capital After 27-Year Run
items.summary: Hedge fund veteran Ricky Sandler is shuttering Eminence Capital and returning investor cash after a 27-year run, telling clients it hasn’t performed well enough in recent years to keep operating.

items.headline: US Life Insurers Have Shifted More General Account Risk Offshore
items.summary: US life insurers have shifted more of their general account risk to entities abroad than in the US as of year-end, the first time that offshore reinsurance hubs have overtaken domestic ones for that business.

items.headline: Tax the Rich Policies Draw Attention
items.summary: Michael Bologna, Bloomberg Industry Group senior tax correspondent, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Democrats around the country are pushing forward on plans to tax the rich — moves that are supportive for the tax-exempt municipal-bond market.   (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Fmr. Fed Governor Betsy Duke on Warsh's Future, Rates
items.summary: Former Federal Reserve Governor Betsy Duke discusses the Department of Justice dropping its investigation into Jerome Powell, what a possible Kevin Warsh led Fed could look like, and previews Wednesday’s rate decision. She talks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Private Credit Needs to Close the Trust Gap: Kasser
items.summary: Susan Kasser, head of private debt at Neuberger, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The amount of new money private credit managers brought in during the first quarter plunged almost 60% from a year earlier, an added challenge for funds that are also grappling with unprecedented redemption requests. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: 'Pockets of Complacency' in Credit: Barings' Best
items.summary: Michael Best, high yield portfolio manager at Barings, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Attractive yields and tight spreads produced a supply surge in the US high yield market this week. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Brazil Blocks Polymarket, Kalshi Over ‘Illegal Betting’
items.summary: Brazil’s government blocked access to Polymarket and Kalshi, part of a crackdown on prediction market platforms it said were out of compliance with federal gambling laws.

items.headline: Belgium Cut by S&P in Second Judgment on Worst Euro-Zone Deficit
items.summary: Belgium was cut by S&P Global Ratings, the second downgrade by a credit assessor in a week on the country running the euro area’s biggest budget deficits.

items.headline: Wall Street Conviction Frays on Trades Riding Out AI, War Shock
items.summary: One by one, high-conviction trades on Wall Street are falling out of favor.

items.headline: Nasdaq 100, S&P 500 Finish at Record on Intel’s Blowout Results
items.summary: US stocks finished at all-time highs on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks, while trader optimism around the prospect of US-Iran talks increased.

items.headline: Record Day for S&P, Nasdaq | Closing Bell
items.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Iran War Impact on Credit Markets | Real Yield 4/24/2026
items.summary: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: BMO Capital Markets Head of US Rates Strategy Ian Lyngen, Moody's Rating Global Head of Private Credit Marc Pinto, Former Federal Reserve General Counsel Scott Alvarez, Barings High Yield Portfolio Manager Michael Best, and Neuberger Head of Private Debt Susan Kasser.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: ‘The Jig Is Up’: 70% Crash Marks Quick End to Avis Short Squeeze
items.summary: The speculative mania around Avis Budget Group Inc. erupted quickly. It collapsed even faster.

items.headline: Amazon-Backed X-Energy Climbs 27% After $1.02 Billion US IPO
items.summary: X-Energy Inc. shares closed 27% above their IPO price, after the nuclear energy firm raised $1.02 billion in an upsized US initial public offering.

items.headline: Nvidia Breakout Sends Chip Giant to First Record Since October
items.summary: Nvidia Corp. shares rose to their first record in months on Friday, the latest example of improving momentum at the leader in artificial intelligence chips.

items.headline: Stocks Hit Record Highs on US-Iran Talk Optimism: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Hopes that the US and Iran will be back to the negotiating table lifted stocks to all-time highs, with the market also rising after a blowout forecast from Intel Corp. and the closing of a probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

items.headline: Oil Declines on Renewed Optimism for US-Iran Negotiations
items.summary: Oil prices fell on fresh hopes that elusive peace talks between the US and Iran may materialize after all and pave the way for the resumption of energy flows though the vital Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Copper and Silver Miner Lumina’s Toronto IPO Raises $297 Million
items.summary: Lumina Metals Corp. and one of its shareholders raised C$406.2 million ($297 million) in an upsized Canadian initial public offering.

items.headline: VantageScore CEO: Mortgage, Housing Industry in Crisis
items.summary: Vantagescore President & CEO Silvio Tavares discusses the impacts of Fannie Mae & Freddie Mac moving to implement Vantagescore 4.0 and the rising competition in the US mortgage market. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Treasuries Gain as DOJ Drops Fed Probe, Opening Path for Warsh
items.summary: Treasuries gained for the first time in a week after the Justice Department dropped its investigation into the Federal Reserve, potentially opening a path for President Donald Trump’s pick Kevin Warsh to become Fed chair and push for interest-rate cuts.

items.headline: Goldman Is Avoiding Charity Cases In ETF Market-Making Return
items.summary: The Wall Street giant is stepping back in after years on the sidelines—but only for funds it thinks can “reach escape velocity,” says Ashok Varadhan.

items.headline: Fmr. Fed General Counsel Discusses DOJ's Powell Probe
items.summary: Scott Alvarez, former general counsel at the Federal Reserve Board and adjunct professor at the George Washington School of Law, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The Justice Department is ending a controversial investigation into building-renovation cost overruns at the Federal Reserve, potentially clearing a path to confirmation for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s pick to be the next chair of the central bank. (Source: Bloomberg)
